Methanoic acid =8 °C, Ethanoic acid =17 °C, Propanoic acid =2 °C, Butanoic acid =-6 °C, Pentonoic acid =-35 °C, Hexonic acid =-20 °C, Heptanoic acid =-8 °C, Octanoic acid =17 °C acid. Water solubility of carboxylic acid decreases as we increase the molar mass & hence hydrophobic part of the chain.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A broad-spectrum antibiotic is an antibiotic that acts on the two major bacterial groups, gram-positive and gram-negative, or any antibiotic that acts against a wide range of disease-causing bacteria.

Penicillin G has a narrow spectrum. Ampicillin and Amoxycillin are synthetic modifications of penicillins. These have broad spectrum. 

Chloramphenicol is a broad spectrum antibiotic. It is rapidly absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract and hence can be given orally in case of typhoid, dysentery, acute fever, certain form of urinary infections, meningitis and pneumonia.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cr2O72-+OH-⟶2CrO42-

Let the oxidation number of chromium be x

The oxidation state of chromium in Cr2O72- =2x+(-2)7=-2x=+6

Oxidation state of Cr in CrO42-  

=x+(-2)4=-2x=+6.

So, the oxidation number of Cr in CrO42- is also +6.

Hence, the difference in oxidation number between ions is 0

solution:
Calamine is the carbonate ore of zinc.It composition is ZnCO3.

Galena is also called lead glance is the natural mineral form of lead (ii) sulfide,PbS

Sphalerite is a sulfide mineral with the formula ZnS. It is the most important ore of zinc.

Siderite is a mineral composed of iron(II) carbonate (FeCO3).

In first step nitration of benzene ring will take place at para position, since ethyl group is ortho-para directing in nature. 

Then halogenation reaction will take place at benzyl position followed by dehydrohalogenation reaction with alcoholic KOH.

The largest single use of dihydrogen is in the synthesis of ammonia (compound of nitrogen) which is used in the manufacture of nitric acid and and nitrogenous fertilizers like urea.

Around 55% of hydrogen around the world would goes to ammonia production.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
B2H6 have four 2c-2e bonds and two 3c-2e bonds. Bridging bonds have larger bond length than terminal bonds. Angle between terminal bonds is more than angle between bridging bonds if all 4 terminal bonds are in one plane then bridging bonds are in perpendicular plane.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Metallic oxides are basic in nature, Oxides of non-metals are mostly acidic in nature and some are neutral or amphoteric.

Among the given oxides Na2O is a metallic oxide, and it is basic in nature.

Cl2O7 is a non-metal oxide and it is acidic in nature.

N2O and NO are non-metallic oxides but are neutral in nature.

As2O3 is an Amphoteric oxide. It reacts with both acids and bases.

So, The number of amphoteric oxides is 1.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The size of halogen increases from F to I. The N-F bond is stronger than other N-X bonds due to the high electronegativity of fluorine. Larger the size of the halogen, less is the stability of nitrogen halide:

The stability order is NF3>NCl3>NBr3>NI3.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Calcium phosphate is the major component of hydroxyapatite crystal that form the inorganic portion of the teeth

Calcium phosphate is a mineral comprising calcium ion (Ca2+) and phosphate ion P+5. It is present in the crown area of the tooth and also in bones. It contain some F- ions too. But it does not contain P+3 ions.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Poly β-hydroxybutyrate-co- β-hydroxy valerate (PHBV) : It is obtained by the copolymerisation of 3-hydroxybutanoic acid and 3-hydroxypentanoic acid.

PHBV is used in speciality packaging, orthopaedic devices and in controlled release of drugs. PHBV undergoes bacterial degradation in the environment.

solution:
The orbitals which have same energy are called degenerate orbitals. Energy or an orbital depends on two quantum numbers, principle quantum number (n) and azimuthal quantum number (l).

The orbitals with similar values of n & l but with different value of m will be degenerate orbitals.

Among the given set or orbital only option (B) fulfil this condition.

solution:
Oil and water are not soluble in each other. since oils are non-polar organic compounds and water is a polar compound. So, they will form hydrophobic type of colloid.

Hydrophobic collides are unstable and required some stabilisers like proteins, gums, natural and synthetic soaps, etc. Excess of electrolyte can not act as stabilisers.

So, statement 1 is true but statement 2 is false.

dAdt=8πrdrdt=k (let)    ...1

On integrating on both sides w.r.t t , we get

Also given that, at t=5, r=7

⇒4π×49=5k+36π

⇒5k=4π49-9   ⇒5k=4π×40 

⇒k=32π

On substituting k value in equation 1we get, 4πr2=32πt+36π

⇒r2=8t+9

Given t=9.

⇒r2=81⇒r=9.

We know that, cos-1x is defined when -1⩽x⩽1

So, cos-1x2-5x+6x2-9 is defined when

-1⩽x2-5x+6x2-9⩽1 and x2-9≠0

 ⇒-1≤x-3x-2x-3x+3≤1 and x≠±3

⇒-1≤1-5x+3≤1 and x≠±3, 

⇒-2≤-5x+3⩽0 and x≠±3

⇒0≤5x+3⩽2 and x≠±3, ⇒x+3≥52 and x≠±3

⇒ x≥-12 and x≠±3

∴ x∈-12,∞-3  ⋯1

Now, we know that logx is defined for x>0.

So, logx2-3x+2 is defined for x2-3x+2>0, ⇒x-1x-2>0

⇒x>2 or x<1 ⋯2

from equations 1 & 2

∴ x∈-12,1∪2,∞-3

Using fundamental principle of counting,

when fc=0, fa=1 then  fb-fd=2 has 7 choices,

Similarly when fc=0, fa=2 then fb-fd has 5 choices.

Counting like this we get the total number of one-one function as 31

3λ-2μ+7,-λ-3μ-6,λ-μ-2

Now, 

3λ-2μ+71=-λ-3μ-6-4=λ-μ-22

i.e. -12λ+8μ-28=-λ-3μ-6

⇒λ=μ-2

Also 

-2λ-6μ-12=-4λ+4μ+8

⇒λ=5μ+10

After solving above two equations, we get λ=-5,μ=-3

i.e. A=-8,6,-7 and B=-6,-2,-3

Hence, AB2=4+64+16=84

subject: Mathematics
solution:
D.R. 's of a→1=-1,0,3

D.R. 's of a→2=0,-1,2

D.R. 's of b→1=1,-a,0 

D.R. 's of b→2=1,-1,1 

Now, a→2-a→1=i^-j^-k^

and b→1×b→2=i^j^k^1-a01-11

b→1×b2→=i^-a-j^+k^a-1

i.e. b1→×b2→=a2+1+a-12

So, a→2-a→1·b→1×b→2=2-2a

Shortest distance between the lines, 21-aa2+1+a-12=23

Squaring on both the sides, we get,

31-a2=a2-a+1

 i.e. a=2,12

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given sin θ tan θ+tan θ=sin 2θ; θ≠±π2

⇒sin2 θcos θ+sin θcos θ=2 sin θ cos θ

⇒sin θ=0 or sin θcos θ+1cos θ=2 cos θ

⇒θ=0, π, -π or sin θ+1=21-sin2 θ

⇒θ=0, π, -π or 2 sin2 θ +sin θ-1=0

⇒θ=0, π, -π or sin θ=12, sin θ=-1

⇒θ=0, π, -π or θ=π6, 5π6 or θ=-π2 (rejected)

Hence, θ=0, π, -π, π6, 5π6

⇒nS=5

Now, T=Σcos 2θ

=cos 0+cos 2π+cos-2π+cosπ3+cos5π3

=1+1+1+12+12=4

So, T+nS=9

subject: Physics
solution:
The current in AC is of the form of i=i0sinωt. The maximum current is i0 and rms current is i02. The change in the phase when current change from maximum to rms value is π4 rad. Therefore, the time taken for the same will be,

t=∆ϕω=π4×2π×50=2.5 ms.

subject: Physics
solution:
The reactance of the given inductor is,

XL=ωL=2πfL=2π×50×0.2=20π Ω.

The maximum current in the given circuit will be,

i0=2VrmsXL=2×22020π=242π A

Therefore, a=242

subject: Physics
options: In the ground state of Rutherford's model electrons are in stable equilibrium. While in Thomson's model electrons always experience a net-force.
An atom has a nearly continuous mass distribution in a Rutherford's model but has a highly non-uniform mass distribution in Thomson's model
A classical atom based on Rutherford's model is doomed to collapse.
The positively charged part of the atom possesses most of the mass in Rutherford's model but not in Thomson's model.
solution:
According to Rutherford's model, most of the mass of the atom is concentrated at the centre (nucleus) and the electron revolves around it.

As the electron is moving in a circular motion, it is accelerated. According to Maxwell accelerated charged particle loses its energy in the form of electro-magnetic radiation.

Therefore, a classical atom based on Rutherford's model is doomed to collapse.

Vmax=3.6×107×d.

Now,

C=qmaxVmax⇒kε0Ad=7×10-73.6×107×dk=dε0×30π×10-4×7×10-73.6×107×d=2.33

subject: Physics
solution:
The wavelength of the carrier wave is,

λc=cνc=3×1083.5×109=335 m.

The minimum size of the antenna required is 14th of the wavelength of the carrier. Therefore,

l=14×335=21.4 mm

subject: Physics
solution:
Equivalent resistance of the parallel 20 Ω branches will be 10 Ω.

Equivalent EMF of the two branches which are in parallel will be, =1.5r+1.5r1r+1r=1.5 V and the equivalent internal resistance for same branches will be, r2.

For loop containing voltmeter and 10 Ω resistor, 1.2-10i=0 ⇒i=0.12 A.

Reading of voltmeter should be,

1.2=1.5-ir2 ⇒ir2=0.3 ⇒r=0.6i=0.60.12= 5 Ω

subject: Physics
solution:
If the potential difference between the potentiometer is V and the potential of the battery is ε. 

Then, εV=lL

Therefore, ε1ε2=l1l2⇒32=75x⇒x=50 cm

Therefore, the difference in the balancing length is 75-50=25 cm

subject: Physics
solution:
Maximum kinetic energy is expressed as Kmax=hf-ϕ, here, ϕ is work function of metal.

For first case, 12mv12=h2f0−f0

For second case, 12mv22=h5f0−f0

⇒v22v12=41⇒v2v1=2

subject: Physics
solution:
The speed of light in a given medium is given by,

v=cμrεr

Magnetic field is B=μrμ0H.

Also, the relation between the magnetic field and electric field is given by,

E=vB⇒E=cμrεr×μrμ0H=cεr×μr×μ0H⇒E=3×1086.44×1.61×4π×10-7×4.5×10-2=8.48 V m-1

subject: Physics
solution:
To balance gravitational force, electric force on the liquid drop should act in upward direction.

For translational equilibrium along vertical direction,

qE=mg

⇒q=mgE=0.1×10-3×9.84.9×105=2.0×10-9C

subject: Physics
solution:
The variation of the acceleration due to gravity with height h is given by,

g'=g1+hR2.

Since the weight at given height is 13 of the weight at the surface

⇒g'=g3⇒g1+hR2=g3⇒1+hR=3⇒h=R3-1=6400×1.732-1=4685 km

subject: Physics
solution:
The RMS speed of the molecule is given by vRMS=3RTM. Therefore, to double the speed the temperature must become 4 times.

Therefore, the change in the temperature is ∆T=3T0.

Now the process is isochoric (enclosed vessel) therefore, the heat required will be,

H=nCv∆T⇒H=5628×52R×3×273+127=12000 cal=12 kcal

subject: Physics
solution:
Given initial velocity u=9.8 m s-1.

Acceleration a=μg=0.5×9.8=4.9 m s-2

Using, v2=u2+2as, here, final velocity v=0.

So, 0=9.82+24.9s⇒s=9.8 m

subject: Physics
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.
solution:
The force experienced by the charge particle is perpendicular to the instantaneous velocity at all instants as F→=qv→×B→.

Hence, the magnetic force cannot bring any change in the speed of the charged particle. Since, the speed remains constant, the kinetic energy also remains constant.

subject: Physics
solution:
Magnetic field at the centre of the circle is, B=μ0i2R

Magnetic field at the point on the axis is, 

B2=μ0iR22R2+x232⇒B2=μ0iR22R2+R2232⇒B2=μ0i25432R=253B
